section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports tenderness to palpation over the medial joint line of the right knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a symptom description reported by the patient, not an objective exam finding documented by the clinician.
Laxity here is described as a patient-reported symptom rather than an observed physical exam finding.
Joint clicking is being documented as a patient-reported symptom, which belongs in the history rather than the objective findings section.
This is a patient denial of a symptom, which is part of the subjective history rather than an exam or test result.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor will examine the patient's knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: An exam is typically performed before the plan; documenting a future exam in the plan is somewhat disorganized, though it does not substantially change clinical meaning.

A recommendation of amputation would normally follow from and be justified in the assessment; placed directly in the plan without corresponding diagnostic rationale is structurally incongruent, though clearly recognizable as a (likely erroneous) treatment plan item.
